title: ugetuopenflag
text:
ugetuopenflag Get the address of the flag that specifies the validity of the Oracle Logon Data Area (LDA). unsigned char* ugetulda (); Copy Parameters None Return Values A pointer to an unsigned char variable that contains the status. Use Oracle only, uora NN (your currently used Oracle driver). Description ugetuopenflag returns a pointer to an unsigned char variable that contains the validity of the Oracle LDA returned by ugetulda . If that variable is 1 (one), the LDA returned by ugetulda is valid; if it is zero, the LDA is invalid and should not be used. Note: You should always call this function before using the LDA returned by ugetulda in Oracle OCI calls. If you fail to do so, you might be using an invalid LDA, which is likely to produce unexpected results. Obviously, you can call this function only when an Oracle driver is available to your application. Example: ugetulda and ugetuopenflag The following 3GL code drops the table FOOBAR from the current Oracle database. Functionally, this might not be the best example, but it is simple and serves to illustrate the use of ugetulda and ugetuopenflag . The routine DROPTABLE can be called, using perform or activate , once the application has logged on to Oracle. Explain this code #include <h3gl.h> #include <stdio.h> #include <ocidem.h> EXPORT void DROPTABLE () { unsigned char *pflg; Lda_Def *plda; Cda_Def cda; pflg = ugetuopenflag(); if ( *pflg != 1 ) { printf("No valid LDA
"); return; } plda = (Lda_Def*)ugetulda(); if ( oopen(&cda, plda, NULL, -1, -1, NULL, -1) ) { printf("Open cursor failed
"); return; } if ( oparse(&cda, (text *)"DROP TABLE FOOBAR", -1, 0, 2) ) { printf("Drop table failed, Oracle error = %d
", cda.rc); return; } printf("Drop table OK.
"); } Copy Use the following command to link the shared library on Solaris 64-bits. Note the explicit linking of the U4.2 Oracle driver; this was tested with Oracle 8.1.7. Explain this code cc -g -KPIC -xtarget=ultra -xarch=v9 -G \ -I 3gl/include \ -I $ORACLE_HOME/rdbms/demo \ -o lib/libdroptable.so \ -L./lib -luora42 \ droptable.c Copy Related Links ugetulda Get the address of the Oracle logon data area (LDA) of the first Oracle logon path.
